__fixture__.name: extension_quarantine_suspect_host
__fixture__.scenario: Extension or runtime host exceeded its restart budget. Supervisor quarantined the suspect host; bisect proposes a narrower candidate without ever silently re-enabling an extension or mutating marketplace state.

title: Extension quarantine for suspect host regression
summary: Supervisor quarantined a suspect extension host after exceeding its restart budget; bisect narrows the candidate without mutating marketplace state.
preconditions: Supervisor observed extension_regression_suspected or crash_loop_detected on extension_and_tool_hosts within the strike budget.
Doctor emitted doctor.finding.extension_host_crash_loop_quarantined or doctor.finding.extension_activation_budget_exceeded with confidence_class observed_authoritative or observed_with_gap.

linkage_bindings.notes: current_rung_class = extension_quarantine when a bundle is exported while this rung is active.
Escalation handoffs cite the rung, the repair_transaction_ref, and the suspect extension id.
The rung is the default destination for the crash-loop quarantine and activation-budget findings.
The quarantine-and-bisect transaction ref binds here; installed_extension_marketplace_state is never mutated.

explanation_fields.preserved_work_summary: Authored files, open buffers, durable indexes, trust, credentials, managed policy, and the installed extension marketplace state all remain untouched. Bisect never silently re-enables a quarantined extension.
explanation_fields.disabled_capability_summary: Extension auto-activation, extension host launch, background rebuild, and AI runtime access stay disabled while the suspect extension is quarantined.
explanation_fields.escalation_summary: Escalate when the same host quarantines within its strike budget, when the user declines bisect, or when a task the user is trying to finish requires a capability this rung has disabled.
explanation_fields.user_facing_next_step: Review the bisect candidate, keep the quarantine, or export a recovery-follow-up handoff packet naming the suspect extension id and host class.
